issue.title: `get_state` reads state.json mid-write — silent JSON parse failures

successCriteria.description: Transient read failures during atomic-write race conditions retry instead of bubbling up as silent frontend errors
Legitimate missing-file case (no state.json yet) still returns Ok(None) without delay
Same fix applies to get_plan (same race pattern for plan files written by /tiki:execute)
cargo check and pnpm build pass with no regressions

phases.title: Add read_json_resilient helper to fs_utils.rs
phases.content:
Add a generic helper `read_json_resilient<T: DeserializeOwned>(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<T>, String>` to `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/fs_utils.rs`.

Behavior:
1. If `path.exists()` returns false, return `Ok(None)` immediately (fast path for legitimate missing-file case — no retry, no delay).
2. Otherwise, attempt up to 3 reads with 25ms sleep between attempts.
3. On each attempt: try `read_to_string` then `serde_json::from_str::<T>`. Return `Ok(Some(parsed))` on success.
4. On NotFound errors mid-read (file disappeared during a rename), retry. On parse errors, retry. On other read errors, retry.
5. After all attempts fail, return `Err` with the last error message including which attempt failed and why.
6. Log each retry at warn level for diagnosability.

Imports needed:
- `use serde::de::DeserializeOwned;`
- `use std::time::Duration;` (already imported as crate uses chrono — verify)

The helper handles three real race conditions during atomic writes:
- File temporarily missing between exists() check and open
- Windows ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION during MoveFileEx rename
- Partial JSON readable between write and rename in unusual filesystem states
verification: fs_utils.rs contains `pub fn read_json_resilient<T: DeserializeOwned>` definition
Function returns Ok(None) immediately when path doesn't exist
Function retries up to 3 times with 25ms sleep on read or parse failures
Function logs each retry at warn level

phases.title: Refactor get_state and get_plan to use read_json_resilient
phases.content:
Update `apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs` so that `get_state` and `get_plan` delegate their file read + parse logic to the new `fs_utils::read_json_resilient` helper.

Changes:
1. `get_state` (around line 39): replace the existence check + `read_to_string` + `from_str` block (lines 50-58) with a single call to `fs_utils::read_json_resilient::<TikiState>(&state_file)`.
2. `get_plan` (around line 63): replace the equivalent block (lines 74-82) with `fs_utils::read_json_resilient::<TikiPlan>(&plan_file)`.
3. Both functions retain their existing path-resolution logic (tiki_path argument or fall back to current_dir).

This closes the same race for plan reads since `/tiki:execute` rewrites plan files between phases and the Active Work sidebar reads them on watcher events.
verification: No direct read_to_string + serde_json::from_str pairs remain in get_state/get_plan
Existing path-resolution behavior preserved (tiki_path arg or current_dir fallback)
